Currently on my second of a three night stay. Overall, I'm pretty pleased so far.
-It was really easy to get to from the airport and most everything you need is a short drive away.
-There are plenty of parking spots for the complimentary parking and it's hassle-free.
-When booking, I opted for the more expensive "breakfast included" option. There isn't a free, continental breakfast type of option. Instead, if you opt for the breakfast, you get a voucher to use at the restaurant/bar that's on premises. The daily increased cost for this option is about the same as if you were to just buy from the menu outright. Food was great!
-There's a small indoor pool and gym if you're into that.
-The sundries shop is actually really reasonable. I bought some nail clippers, expecting to be charged $14 (it's happened to me elsewhere) but they only charged $2.
-This is probably the first time I've felt confident that a hotel bed was actually clean.
-AC works great, which is important to me as I hate the heat.

Best of all, the staff has been extremely courteous and helpful. If I ever find myself back in Colorado Springs, I'll definitely stay here again.

We spent two nights at this hotel. What made it such a great experience - was the staff! Everyone was so helpful, kind, thoughtful and friendly.

When I arrived, the front desk clerk told me there would be a $20 incidental hold, refundable at checkout, which was fine. She then charged me for the full stay AND the $20 hold, and said I could use Apple Pay. The transaction showed as processed on my end, and I was given my room keys.

A few minutes later, I received a call from the front desk saying my payment “didn’t go through.” That was not true, because Apple Pay clearly showed it processed. Then the manager, Tamika, came out and told me their system “does not work with Apple Pay” — something that should’ve been mentioned before processing the transaction. Because I didn’t have my physical card with me, I had to pull up the card virtually, and she manually typed in the card number to charge me again. By the next morning, I noticed multiple charges from Holiday Inn on my account. To make matters worse, when I returned to my room later that day, my key had been deactivated because they said my stay was only booked for one night. I had to go back to the front desk, explain everything again, and was told they needed to reissue a key or move me to another room.

The entire situation was confusing, unprofessional, and exhausting. I was overcharged several times, had to spend time on the phone with corporate after my stay to get the extra charges removed, and never received a clear explanation from the staff about what went wrong.

This was supposed to be a simple, two-night stay — instead, it turned into a billing mess and unnecessary hassle. I will not be returning to this location and would strongly caution anyone using Apple Pay or expecting straightforward service to think twice before booking here.

The staff were kind, the man who checked us in, Matt, gave us great restaurant recommendations and everyone was incredibly responsive to any requests during our 5 night stay. You can basically watch the sunset over the mountains from the parking lot, a ton of great restaurants within walking distance and even more that will deliver to your room door. My only gripes were the elevator was a bit scary the first time we used it (it will open just give it a second) as it is definitely older, and (my own fault) we GPSd our way to the other Holiday Inn Express in Longmont more than once haha. I travel often worldwide and I rarely write reviews, even rarer for me to feel compelled to write a strictly positive review but I HIGHLY recommend this hotel, even for our family of 5 in one room, it was a comfortable, spacious and leisurely stay. And the price tag of our trip could’ve been SO much higher for less amenities if we hadn’t decided to give this one a try.

Our room- on the third floor, had a running toilet which was constantly making noise, both the faucet and drain were dripping, and there was hair in the shower walls. Our outlets to charge our devices didn’t work, we had to charge our electronics on the first floor.

The breakfast was missing many high quality items, no eggs, no cinnamon rolls, no yogurt, one cereal and a few breads and muffins. For us, we look forward to this breakfast as it’s usually the highlight of our stay.

We hope that this holiday inn gets some much needed upgrades and changes so that we can come back, until then we will be giving our business to the other higher quality hotels nearby.

She immediately stated that reviewing the cameras would not be helpful because she “didn’t know what time it happened” and claimed she could not review footage from the entire night.

I offered to review the footage myself and explained that the police would likely need to review it anyway. She refused and repeatedly insisted that even if a car was seen near mine, it could not be assumed to be responsible, suggesting the tail light cover might have fallen off earlier and that the driver could have been “just passing by.” Throughout the conversation, she continued to defend the unknown driver rather than support a guest whose vehicle had been damaged on hotel property.

Given her response, I told her clearly that her handling of the situation was unacceptable and asked that she speak with my husband instead, as I was no longer willing to continue the conversation. I then stepped away and sat in the lobby area.

While seated nearby, I could still hear the manager speaking loudly about the situation to the front desk staff, making dismissive and mocking comments, as if to justify herself and minimize my concern. This behavior felt highly inappropriate and unprofessional, especially considering the stress of the situation.

This reasoning was especially unreasonable given the location. This is a quiet mountain area with virtually no activity overnight. The parking lot was nearly empty, and it is highly unlikely that multiple vehicles were coming and going throughout the night. Any vehicle moving near mine would have stood out clearly.

What makes this situation even more troubling is that later that same morning, the police located the responsible driver, who was arrested for DUI and hit and run. The tail light cover found next to my car matched his vehicle exactly. In other words, the incident the manager refused to take seriously or investigate was completely legitimate.

I expect far more from someone in a management position. When a guest experiences property damage on hotel grounds, the response should involve support, professionalism, and a willingness to assist, not dismissal, excuses, or public commentary about the guest. Because of the manager’s behavior and lack of accountability, this stay was unacceptable.

I would not stay here again and cannot recommend this hotel while this type of management behavior continues.

Check-in took longer than expected but the staff were very courteous and the rooms were clean. Location and price were much better than other hotels in the area. Will definitely try to stay here again on our next trip.

So you need to take this into consideration, especially if you are bringing heavy luggage or if you need accessibility. If you are after the view, I really can’t recommend it as my group and I really didn’t find it breathtaking enough to justify us dragging our luggage to our room. Also, if you are a group of 3 or more and opted to book a room with breakfast, do note that their breakfast would only cover 2 people.

I recently had the pleasure of staying with my family at the Holiday Inn in Estes Park, Colorado, and I couldn’t have asked for a better location. Conveniently located right near the Rocky Mountain National Park, the hotel offers breathtaking mountain views and is just minutes from the park entrance. It’s also close to downtown Estes Park, making it easy to explore local shops, restaurants, and trails without having to drive far. Whether you’re visiting for hiking, wildlife viewing, or simply to enjoy the fresh mountain air, this hotel puts you right where you want to be. During the summer time, there’s a free shuttle (Silver Route) that runs through 9pm.

What truly set this stay apart, though, was the exceptional customer service. From check-in to check-out, the staff went above and beyond to ensure our comfort. Everyone was warm, welcoming, and genuinely helpful—offering personalized recommendations for local dining and hikes, and promptly addressing any request we had. Their local knowledge and professionalism made us feel right at home, and it was clear that guest satisfaction is a top priority at this property. Mr. Alexis, Operations Manager, was very engaged and was super helpful. He personally congratulated me on a special occasion we were celebrating. The staff, including Jeff, EJ, and others were fantastic too.

The amenities were another highlight of our stay. The hotel features a well-equipped fitness center, a relaxing indoor pool and hot tub, a family game room, and spacious, modern rooms that were spotless and comfortable. The on-site restaurant served an excellent hot breakfast buffet and evening meals with a great variety of options. We also appreciated the free parking and reliable Wi-Fi, which made everything more convenient. All in all, the Holiday Inn at Estes Park combines the perfect mountain location with top-tier service and amenities—I’d highly recommend it to anyone planning a trip to the area.

Hotel location was great - about a 15 minute walk (5 minute drive) to the touristy downtown area of Estes Park and a 20 minute drive to Rocky Mountain National Park. The exterior looked a little tired and worn with the lot needing to be seal coated and re-striped. The reception and lobby appeared to have been recently updated and had a nice ambiance, lighting and seating areas. The hotel provided a Bevi machine and free coffee and tea, available at all times of the day in the lobby area. In addition, the fire pits and complimentary s’more kits were a nice added touch. 

We didn’t use the pool, but it looked very nice although I would not want to be in the rooms which overlooked the pool just from a noise  / ambiance perspective. The hotel also had a gaming room with some arcade games and a pool / ping pong table. 

Breakfast was not included, but there is an on-site restaurant and bar with room service as an amenity. There were so many good breakfast places around the area that while free breakfast is a great perk and a cost benefit to families, it wasn’t a major issue for two of us. 

We did have some issues with accessibility due to the elevator from the ground floor to the 1st floor hallway being inoperable so we had to carry our luggage up stairs to access the hallway where the elevator to our 2nd floor room was located. We did relay this issue to staff and it appeared to have been remedied the following day. There did not appear to be a freight/staff elevator so elevator traffic when we were trying to check out along with housekeeping was a little high. 

The room itself appears to have had interior upgrades with newer millwork, lighting fixtures near the beds and carpeting. The immediate issue we noticed was a mildew smell that didn’t go away with the AC set to dry mode and most likely due to lack of ventilation in the bathrooms. The smell was lingering where if you left the room and came back you could smell it but you eventually became sort of “nose blind” to it. Bathroom vanities were newer but not a lot of room to spread out toiletries and the bathtub shower was in need of a full replacement. You could see that they installed newer plastic tub / shower inserts but there was mildew on the shower curtains and unfortunately 
never aired out fully. 

AC was very loud, so ear plugs are recommended. Trash can liners were not installed in the trash cans when we arrived so the room condition on immediate first impression was not great. In addition, the room was an older style where you have a door between if you wanted to share adjoining rooms. The noise buffer / acoustics between rooms was almost non-existent and we had a family with smaller children so we could definitely hear all conversations.  

Overall, 3/5 stay with the bathroom and room utilities being the main detractors.

Room: 3/5

Overall stay for value: 3/5

One of our vendors had to use their own credit card on an already paid for room and another vendor got assigned a room belonging to my bride's brothers resulting in them having to rebook a room when they arrived at a higher rate. Unfortunately, if we had more communication with Holiday Inn from the start, these issues would not have arose.

This hotel meets or exceeds my expectations in every respect. The rooms are spacious and have every convenience I need, with sufficient plugs and USB ports. The breakfast is very good, with cheese omelets, turkey sausage, fruit, juices, DIY pancakes, and those wonderful cinnamon rolls. 2% milk, rather than whole, is a plus. The indoor pool is nice, and the hot tub is large and delightfully warm. They have a small fitness center that is open 24/7, and a large eating area with lots of tables that are available for all day. Breakfast hours are a generous 6-10. Just a great hotel. But it's not easy to see from the nearest major street (Harmony) so it's easy to miss without a map (or Google Maps).

The staff at this hotel is very accommodating. Lucy, Essence, Ryan, Melissa and Charles were all very friendly and helpful. They assisted with our specific needs and even helped us with a room change due to noise from the front doors. The initial room had a terrible paint/chemical smell and they moved us to the second floor, which was right over the doors unfortunately. There was a light out in the tub and Charles, their maintenance staff member, quickly resolved it.

The design of the building is very shallow so the two automatic doors are right next to the front desk, which causes a lot of false openings from guests waiting to check in, produces a lot of noise and vibration for rooms in proximity. Consider this if you're a light sleeper.

There is only one elevator. We only had to wait a few times though. However, there isn't much room in there for a luggage cart and associated family/guests.

We intended to use the pool but upon opening the door, the clorox smell was over powering. It is indoors and conveniently accessed.

The sundry offering is quite small and a regular candy bar is about $2.75. Probably not bad for last minute sweet tooths. However, they only stock it once a month.

Speaking of food, we were there 11 days and we never saw bacon on the menu, which was disappointing. They rotated skillet potatoes in every other day or so, which were a nice change of options. For an included breakfast, it wasn't terrible, but would have been nice to see bacon at least. One good part is being a late sleeper, I was still able to get breakfast up until 930am weekdays and up until 10am on Saturdays. The staff who handles breakfast stays on top of restock fairly well it seemed.

They do allow pets and were very reasonable compared to other Holiday Inn Express' in the area for pet fees, especially if you stay more than a week as we did.

If you're looking for a lot of TV channel options, it's not a huge list, but we were able to hook up the Apple TV to extend streaming, etc. I had some initial issues getting the Wifi authenticated, but staff gave me a phone number for IHG tech help desk and they whitelisted my device and even bumped my bandwidth a little. After that, rarely had a streaming buffering issue if at all.

If you're looking to catch a movie, you can't beat the short walk to the Cinemark, which is quite nice, and may have restored my impression of movie theaters. Plus, you're close to many shopping and food options with short walks or drives.

Overall, the service and guest experience is what I've come to expect and appreciate from being an IHG member choosing Holiday Inn Express as my go to option quite regularly.

Stayed here for business in Room 424 (King Suite) and had a great experience overall. The room was spacious, clean, and well-organized, perfect for a work trip. Only design flaw: the light switch for the kitchenette is by the main door, so you have to walk back out to turn the lights off at night—bit inconvenient but not a deal-breaker.

Breakfast is the most unique I’ve had at a Holiday Inn Express—they sometimes offer hot tortillas and hash browns so you can build your own breakfast burrito. Really enjoyed that touch, but please add salsa. Ketchup and hot sauce don’t cut it if you’re offering burritos. Glad to see the classic cinnamon rolls were back this time too.

Friendly staff, quiet stay, and very good overall value. One warning: be extra careful exiting the parking lot onto Spruce Street—the incline is steep and you will scrape the bottom of your car if you’re not slow and precise.

The hotel itself is clean, comfortable, and beautifully maintained, but what truly stood out to me was the hospitality of the staff. Having worked in the hospitality and customer experience industries for over 25 years, I can confidently say that the service here rivals some of the best.

Lara, in particular, was an absolute gem. She mentioned that this memorial event is her favorite time of year, and her warmth and delight in serving the guests were clear. One small example of her dedication: when my boyfriend asked if there was any ibuprofen available, she graciously offered him some of her personal supply. She explained that she keeps a stock on hand for situations like this—going above and beyond to care for guests. I had a wonderful stay at the Holiday Inn and Suites in Colorado Springs. Here are a few things that I particularly enjoyed about my stay:

Clean and Fresh-Smelling Rooms

One of the things that stood out to me about the Holiday Inn and Suites was how clean and fresh-smelling the rooms were. Compared to other hotel chains, which allow pets and can sometimes have a musty smell as a result, the Holiday Inn and Suites was a breath of fresh air. The rooms were impeccably clean and had a pleasant, hotel-fresh scent that made it feel like a truly relaxing and enjoyable place to stay.

Friendly Front Desk Staff

The lady working the front desk was another highlight of my stay. She greeted me and my two daughters every time we walked in the front doors, from check-in to check-out. Her friendly and welcoming demeanor made us feel right at home and added to the overall positive atmosphere of the hotel.

Thorough Housekeeping

The housekeeping staff at the Holiday Inn and Suites were also top-notch. They did a thorough job of cleaning our room each day, making sure that every detail was taken care of and that we had everything we needed for a comfortable stay.

Delicious Breakfast

The breakfast at the Holiday Inn and Suites was another highlight. They offered a wide variety of options, including eggs, bacon, sausage, pancakes, and more. Everything was cooked nicely and tasted delicious, making it the perfect way to start each day of our stay.

Pool and Hot Tub

Finally, I have to mention the pool and hot tub. We swam in them every single day and loved every minute of it. The pool was the perfect size for swimming laps or just splashing around, and the hot tub was the perfect way to relax and unwind after a long day of sightseeing.

Overall, I would highly recommend the Holiday Inn and Suites in Colorado Springs to anyone looking for a clean, comfortable, and enjoyable place to stay. From the friendly staff to the delicious breakfast and the wonderful pool and hot tub, there was so much to love about this hotel.

We received a text message from management that animals are not allowed in their rooms. They had reports of a dog barking in our room and when the manager went by our room they could hear it barking. We didn't bring a dog so this was very concerning. They told us a $250 charge was being placed on our card since we brought a dog in. We responded to the text message from management and informed them we didn't have a dog and they can go in our room to double check. We rushed back to the hotel to also make sure a dog wasn't placed in our room. Upon arrival we received another message saying management rechecked the floor and our room and no dog was found. Before we made it back to also check the $250 charge had already went through to our card (and is still pending 7 days later). This was very aggravating as they went off of reports instead of waiting for approval to check and make sure it wasn't us. Once we made it up to our room we heard the dog they were referring to which was 5 doors down. This was definitely a rough way to start our extended stay.

The next suggestion I would recommend is better parental supervision of children 12 and under. Every day we were there a family with multiple children would come down to breakfast. Some days with parents and some days without-- these children were ranging from 6 to 9 years old. There were multiple incidents at the breakfast counter where they were unattended. These children were overfilling the drink dispenser, one child took napkins off another lady's tray as she was preparing food, and/or using the pancake machine with no parents around (not even downstairs with them). This was happening almost every morning.

During the last two evenings of our stay a group of young boys were running up and down the stairs and then sprinting down the hallways--hiding in between doorways to prevent getting caught. This was close to midnight and again they were with no parents. The following day the same group of young boys were down in the pool without supervision--definitely under the age of 14 per the sign posted on the wall. They were running back and forth between the hot tub and then jumping into the 3-4½ foot pool near our family. We made the best of our pool time, but also very concerning had one of them slipped and fell.

Our room was great, customer service was always available, and our kids loved breakfast.

This location is close to a Walmart and a mall. If you are looking for family restaurants look near Northglenn-plenty of options.

I would definitely stay here again in the future. I hope management takes this review into consideration about unsupervised children as a safety concern in the future.

Our issue came in the first night. At 2 am I started hearing water dripping. I went into the bathroom to check and water was pouring from the ceiling into the bathroom. I called the front desk and they said they would send someone up. 10 minutes later security showed up and said he would look into it. It ends up that the person above us fell asleep with the water on in the tub. Now it has been around 20 minutes in the middle of the night. I called back to the front desk asking for a new room. I was told they were sold out and that they couldn't help me. We couldn't stay where we were. I was told to wait and she would get back to me.
Finally 15 minutes later someone came up and they moved us to another room as someone didn't show up. Good thing since I don't know what we would have done without a room. This room was OK but not as nice and not what we paid for. Finally back to sleep.

The next morning I went to speak to the front desk about the issue and to see what they would do for us due to the issue. The person working was not very helpful and said they could give us some IHG points. This was not acceptable. I asked for a manger and was told she was on maternity leave. I asked for someone that could help me. She said someone would be in later in the day. Finally I found someone to help. Our room smelled musty, the carpet didn't feel the cleanest. There was a metal edge at the foot of the bed that stuck out. The mini fridge was in a cabinet underneath the tv and was difficult to get to, as the door regularly got stuck (broke a nail one day trying to open it). Our tv was an outdated  19 inch on a tv stand, and after the first day or so it stopped working. We tried unplugging it, resetting it, everything. It was like 11pm on our last night so we just did without. The bathroom had a fan in it and was "updated", but was pretty small. The area by the hotel is pretty sketchy - we missed the entrance the first time we arrived due to the fence and Taco Bell. The other hotels in the area are dodgy. Parking at this location is also pretty inconvenient - it's off to the side so if you have a lot of luggage, you have to lug it through the lot with zero sidewalk. Next time, we will stick with Thornton.

I stayed at the Holiday Inn Denver–Lakewood for a training in the area and chose to stay overnight so I didn’t have to drive two hours to make an 8:00 AM start time. I was pleasantly surprised by this property, especially having stayed at quite a few Holiday Inns across the country.

The lobby is beautiful and gives off a higher-end feel the moment you walk in. The elevators appear to be brand new—fast, smooth, and easy to use. My room was very comfortable, with a great shower and an overall clean, updated bathroom. For a one-night stay, it checked all the boxes.

My only real complaint is the lack of a complimentary continental breakfast. They have a full-service restaurant on-site, which is nice, but breakfast is not included in the stay. Other Holiday Inns I’ve visited with restaurants still provide continental breakfast, so this felt like a miss. That said, it’s the only downside worth mentioning.

Overall, this was a great stay—clean room, friendly staff, excellent location, and a really impressive lobby. I would absolutely stay here again.

I’ve spent over 40 years on the road supporting the cellular industry, so I’ve seen my share of hotels—good, bad, and forgettable. The Holiday Inn in Lakewood stands out as one of the very best values in the Denver area, especially for repeat business travelers like me.|Our company keeps coming back here, and with good reason. The staff is consistently professional, friendly, and remembers the needs of long-term guests. Rooms are clean, the property well-maintained, and genuinely comfortable—especially the beds, which matter a lot when you’re living out of a suitcase. WiFi is reliable enough for conference calls and file transfers, which not every hotel can claim.|It’s also in a superb location for our work in the Denver metro area, with easy access to downtown, the Tech Center, Golden, and westbound routes toward the foothills. Add in free parking, a solid on-site restaurant and bar, all year round pool, very fair corporate rates, and it’s an easy choice for our team year after year.|If you’re a business traveler looking for a reliable, welcoming home base in Denver, I highly recommend checking out the Holiday Inn Lakewood. I cannot express enough how perfect this venue was for our special event. From start to finish, Leslie Navarro, the Sales and Catering manager went above and beyond to ensure every detail was flawless, making our reunion an unforgettable experience.



**Exceptional Service:** The hotel staff provided exceptional service, accommodating our every need with a smile. From the event planning process to the day of the reunion, their attention to detail and commitment to making our event a success were evident. The team was flexible, understanding, and incredibly supportive, helping us create a warm and welcoming atmosphere for all our guests.



**Beautiful Venue:** The event space was beautifully arranged, offering a comfortable and elegant setting for our gathering. The decor was tasteful, and the setup was exactly what we envisioned. The hotel provided everything we needed, including state-of-the-art AV equipment, making it easy to share memories and presentations during the event.



**Comfortable Accommodations:** Many of our attendees stayed at the hotel, and everyone raved about the quality of their rooms. The accommodations were clean, comfortable, and well-appointed, providing a relaxing retreat after our festivities. The convenient location of the hotel also made it easy for out-of-town guests to explore the local area.



**Delicious Catering:** The catering provided by Austin, Dominic, and Eric, was top-notch. The menu offered a wide variety of options to choose from and everything was delicious.

**Attention to Detail:** What truly set this experience apart was the hotel's attention to detail. They understood the significance of our reunion and worked tirelessly to ensure everything went smoothly. Whether it was arranging special seating for those with mobility issues or coordinating with our event planner, the hotel staff was on top of everything.



Overall, the Holiday Inn Lakewood exceeded our expectations in every way. Our 50-year reunion was a resounding success, and much of that is thanks to the outstanding team at this hotel. If you're looking for a venue that offers exceptional service, beautiful spaces, and a commitment to making your event truly special, I highly recommend the Holiday Inn Lakewood. We couldn’t have asked for a better place to celebrate such an important milestone!

We had booked a room for 2 nights and, during our stay, requested to upgrade to half board for 1 night. While discussing this with the staff, we were clearly told that it included both lunch and dinner (as it was mentioned as “2 meals”). We even reconfirmed this, and the staff assured us it was correct.

On the first day, we had dinner without any issue. However, after having lunch the next day, we were unexpectedly asked to pay. After several discussions, a supervisor stepped in to resolve the matter, but the way it was handled was disappointing. We were told in a rather harsh manner that it was “free like we didn’t pay at all,” which felt unprofessional and dismissive.

Additionally, the complimentary cake mentioned in the Taj FEX offer was not provided smoothly. We had to follow up multiple times—sometimes we were told it would be sent to our room, other times we were asked to come down. When we did, we were told to wait for up to 2 hours, which was inconvenient. Eventually, we gave up.

On a positive note, the rooms were spacious and comfortable, and the housekeeping staff were prompt in delivering amenities.

Overall, while the stay had its comforts, the experience was let down by poor communication and the lack of professionalism at the front desk.

I am a Gold Elite member of IHG. Holiday Inn Dubai Al-Maktoum Airport hotel is the worst so far of all hotels I have stayed. First, this is the only hotel of IHG which asked for advance payment from me. This is the only hotel, where I was not recognised as Gold Elite member - at the check in or at the breakfast table. I was asked to pay instantly from the room service. It seems this particular hotel of IHG does not trust guests. Well appointed room, good food and other amenities are similar to other IHG hotels, but as far as the true hospitality is concerned, this hotel earns zero point out of hundred from me.

I treated myself and my sister to a Valentine’s staycation (Feb 14–15, 2026) at Holiday Inn Dubai Al Maktoum, and we have never felt this special before!
From the warm welcome to the thoughtful little details, everything was perfect. Our room was spacious and elegant, and the housekeeping team surprised us with the cutest towel bear and handwritten notes with little hearts — such a touching gesture. We were also delighted with a Valentine cake and chocolate box. Thank you, Chef Nasser! (Hope to meet you next time!).
Dining was amazing from dinner to breakfast. The international buffet had so many choices. My favorites were the Tom Yum soup, fish fillet, and roasted chicken. Breakfast was one of the best, we loved the chicken sausage, bacon, pancakes, pastries, and fresh fruits. The omelets were perfectly made! It was also lovely meeting the chefs, especially Chef Hennessy.
A big thank you to Ms. Sylvia for her generosity and to Mr. Chadi, the F&B Director, for the amazing dining experience. And to Ms. Nia from the reception of CUISINES for assisting us with her beautiful, positive energy, her cute smile and warm personality made us feel so welcome.
Most especially, heartfelt thanks to Mr. Arjuna for the super warm welcome and for taking such good care of us throughout our stay. He made sure everything was smooth, comfortable, and truly memorable for us. His kindness, attentiveness, and genuine hospitality really stood out. We will never forget how special he made us feel, his warm personality and dedication to guest satisfaction are truly exceptional. He went above and beyond, and that kind of service is rare to find.
The amenities were excellent! Peaceful night pool views, steam and sauna, gym, table tennis, and foosball. There’s also a beautiful Instagrammable outdoor spot at night. It’s quiet, relaxing, and perfect for families too, with a separate slightly covered kids’ pool.
If you’re looking for a peaceful stay away from the busy city — whether for a celebration or just a break — Holiday Inn Dubai Al Maktoum is highly recommended. Truly worth it. We will definitely be back!

If you are looking for a hotel that takes internet infrastructure seriously, this is the place. While most hotels provide a single, congested network that struggles under pressure, this property offers a sophisticated dual-connection system that ensures you stay productive and connected.

They provide two distinct networks: a Standard Line for basic tasks like checking emails and a Dedicated High-Speed Line for heavy-duty professional use. During my stay, the high-speed connection was flawless. I was able to handle intensive video conferencing, manage large-scale data transfers, and run multiple cloud-based applications simultaneously without a single hint of lag or latency.

The stability is truly impressive; even during peak evening hours when other guests were online, the bandwidth remained consistent and lightning-fast. For anyone who works remotely or handles large files, the reliability of this setup is a massive relief. It is easily the fastest and most efficient Wi-Fi I have ever experienced in a hotel setting.

Would you like me to rewrite this to focus specifically on the technical stability for gaming or professional live-streaming?

Diya is helpful

Мы отдыхали в этом отеле в Дубае вместе с папой и остались в полном восторге. Начну с того, что весь персонал отеля невероятно вежливый, добродушный и всегда готов помочь в любой ситуации. Это чувствуется с первых минут пребывания.

Отель мы выбирали, исходя из экономии, так как в период новогодних праздников цены в Дубае были очень высокими. Отель, расположенный недалеко от аэропорта Аль-Мактум, оказался для нас самым оптимальным вариантом. При этом мы совершенно не ощутили никакого дискомфорта или «неполноты» — в отеле было абсолютно всё для комфортного отдыха.

Питание — выше всяких похвал. Отличные завтраки и обеды, очень разнообразная и вкусная еда, мы были в полном восторге. Во время приёмов пищи персонал постоянно подходил, интересовался, всё ли хорошо, предлагал напитки — обслуживание на высшем уровне.

Также большим плюсом стало наличие бесплатных автобусов до метро, пляжа (Марина) и торгового центра. Несмотря на то что отель находится не в центре, добраться можно было без проблем. Автобусы ходят бесплатно (по предварительной записи за 1 день, записаться нужно на ресепшене) в 12:00 и в 15:00. Мы, правда, чаще пользовались такси — поездка до центра обходилась примерно в 100–120 дирхам.
Номера в отеле очень хорошие и полностью укомплектованы: есть утюг, зубные щётки, гель для душа, шампунь, кондиционер и всё необходимое. Мы были приятно удивлены. В отеле всегда очень приятно пахнет, что создаёт особую атмосферу уюта.
На территории отеля есть красивый бассейн с теплой водой. В те дни, когда мы не хотели ехать на пляж, спокойно оставались в отеле, загорали и наслаждались отдыхом.

Отдельно ещё раз хочу отметить персонал — все всегда улыбаются, искренне стараются помочь и сделать ваш отдых максимально комфортным. За всё время проживания у нас не возникло абсолютно никаких проблем.

В итоге могу сказать, что соотношение цены и качества здесь просто отличное. Если для вас принципиально жить именно в центре города — возможно, этот отель не подойдёт. Но если расположение не играет ключевой роли, а важны комфорт, сервис, питание и выгодная цена, то это отличный выбор.

I used points for one reservation and booked the other at the standard rate. Both reservations had the same confirmation number, as they were made simultaneously, and each was for a king room.

When I checked in on Friday, July 4, I was informed that there was only one reservation, the standard rate one, and that the reservation made with points was missing. Despite presenting my confirmation, the front desk clerk insisted there was no record of that reservation and stated that all rooms were fully booked, which forced us to share a single room. Sharing a room with my adult daughter was unacceptable, so I had to seek alternative accommodations for one of us.

After reviewing my IHG account, I saw that my points had been deducted but were later refunded. When I contacted IHG customer service about the points return and the cancellation of my reservation without prior notice, I was told that using points for rooms on the Fourth of July weekend was not allowed. I asked why I was able to book it online despite this restriction, and the representative explained that booking online using points must have been a mistake on the IHG website.

The IHG website allowed me to book a room using points, but IHG/Holiday Inn later deemed this booking impermissible for that weekend. My reservation was canceled without notification, and IHG/Holiday Inn did not take adequate steps to resolve the situation. The only response from customer service was, "Sorry that happened, but there was a suite available that I would have to pay half price for, and it was only available for one night."

It is hard for me to believe that the hotel was entirely full, especially since the parking lot was less than half full. As a loyal IHG Platinum Elite member since 2002, I have never encountered a situation like this. I am extremely dissatisfied with how IHG/Holiday Inn handled this matter—canceling my reservation, failing to inform me of the cancellation, not honoring my reservation, and making no attempt to resolve the issue apart from offering a paid room that was only available for one night. Because of this experience, I will no longer stay at any IHG locations and will be canceling my IHG membership and credit card.

I used the gym two times - both days it was 87 degrees (!), the treadmill was plugged in, but wouldn't work, the elliptical shut off every time I switched directions, and there were no wipes. The staff were actually really good and helpful and the waitress in the restaurant was fabulous (so sweet and friendly and really efficient). We booked thinking there was a restaurant open in the evenings, but it was only open for breakfast. The phone in the room didn't work, and the toilet handle had to be held down for it to flush, but overall room was fine. We did get daily housekeeping, which we did not last time we stayed in Parker at the La Quinta, which was nice. The one time we tried to use the hot tub, it was cold.

This was my second stay at a Holiday Inn property, after a very good experience in Hong Kong, which is why I chose Holiday Inn Express Safa Park for our New Year holiday in Dubai from 29 December 2025 to 9 January 2026. I stayed with my wife and our 3-year-old son, and overall it was a pleasant and comfortable experience.

From the beginning, the staff were very friendly and helpful, which made us feel welcome throughout our stay. One small but very thoughtful detail I really appreciated was the cold water refill machine at the reception — extremely useful, especially in Dubai’s weather.

Our package included breakfast. The quality was good, but the variety was almost the same every day, which felt a bit repetitive during a longer stay.

The rooms were clean and comfortable, suitable for a family stay. However, the furniture and bathroom feel slightly old and would benefit from renovation. That said, everything worked properly and did not affect our comfort.

I would also like to specially recognize one of the cooks at the restaurant who is from Kerala, India. Unfortunately, I didn’t get his name, but he was extremely friendly and welcoming, always greeting us warmly and making us feel at home. Lots of love from the Maldives!

Overall, it’s a nice, family-friendly hotel with great staff and good value for money. I would definitely consider staying at Holiday Inn again in the future.

This was my second stay at a Holiday Inn property, and once again it was a pleasant experience. My first stay was in Hong Kong, which I really liked, and that’s why I chose Holiday Inn Express Safa Park for our New Year holiday in Dubai from 29 Dec 2025 to 9 Jan 2026, traveling with my wife and our 3-year-old son.

The staff were very friendly and helpful, which made our stay comfortable from the beginning. One small but very thoughtful detail I really appreciated was the cold water refill machine at the reception — it was extremely useful, especially in Dubai’s weather.

Our package included breakfast. The quality was good, but the variety was almost the same every day, so it did get a little boring over a long stay.

The rooms were comfortable and clean, suitable for a family stay. However, the furniture and bathroom feel a bit old, and the rooms would benefit from a renovation. That said, everything worked fine and overall it was still a good experience.

Overall, it’s a nice, family-friendly hotel with great staff and good value for money. I would definitely consider staying at Holiday Inn again in the future.

He is very polite

During our stay at this hotel in Dubai, my skirt was damaged by the hotel’s own ironing equipment, which was dirty/defective. As a result, the skirt was burned and permanently stained.

We immediately reported this to the hotel staff. We were told a solution would be provided the same evening. This never happened. The next morning we were again promised follow-up, but no one contacted us.

At check-out, the staff claimed they were unaware of the situation. After waiting over two hours, the manager, Hermano, spoke to us and placed the blame entirely on us, despite the damage being caused by hotel equipment. The way this was handled and the tone used were disrespectful and unprofessional.

In addition to this incident, the room was not clean, and the shower drain was clogged, causing the entire bathroom to flood. These issues further highlight the lack of proper maintenance and attention to basic hygiene.

The only “compensation” offered was a free lunch completely inadequate and irrelevant, as it does not cover the damage caused. No responsibility was taken, no sincere apology was offered, and the situation was handled very poorly.

This experience showed a lack of accountability, communication, and basic customer service. We expected much better from a hotel in Dubai and would not recommend this property based on how guests are treated when something goes wrong.

Reaction:

Dear Mr. Santoso,

Thank you for your response. I appreciate the time taken to reply; however, I must formally disagree with the explanation provided.

Regarding the ironing incident, I would like to clearly state that the skirt in question was suitable for ironing, as explicitly indicated on the care label. This label was shown to your team during our discussion, as we were asked to demonstrate that ironing was permitted, and the hotel was therefore fully aware of this fact. The skirt has been ironed multiple times at home without any issue, using an iron. This directly contradicts the claim that the fabric requires dry-cleaning only or is unsuitable for direct heat.

The damage occurred specifically due to residue or contamination on the hotel-provided iron, not due to misuse or fabric sensitivity. Had the iron been clean and in proper condition, this damage would not have occurred.

While goodwill gestures such as a complimentary lunch or late check-out were mentioned, these do not constitute appropriate compensation for property damage. We were on holiday, and after already waiting more than two hours, in addition to the time lost the evening before, our vacation time had already been significantly impacted. Offering a late check-out assumes that spending additional time “waiting” in the hotel room is desirable, which is not the case under these circumstances. Similarly, a complimentary lunch does not address the damage caused or the loss of valuable time.

What is particularly disappointing is that we now find ourselves having to justify the situation once again. Just as during the initial conversation with you at the hotel, responsibility is being placed back on us, while the facts we presented at the time such as the condition of the iron and the care label are being dismissed or overlooked. Unfortunately, it felt then, and feels now, that these points are not being fully acknowledged.

Выбирали городской отель, около метро, чтобы можно было ездить на экскурсии, прогуляться по городу и по желанию сходить на пляж. Метро было в 800м  пешком минут 10, но станция в час пик очень загруженная т.к. рядом находятся несколько бизнес центров, приходилось буквально выпихиваться из вагона. Если попадать не в час пик, то в целом нормально. Около метро есть продуктовый. Из отеля ходит трансфер на пляж Ля Мэр с пн по сб. Мы ходили пешком до общественного пляжа Джумейра 2, около 35 - 40 минут, дорога нормальная, через частный сектор. Пляж цивильный, душевые и туалеты, магазинчики и ларечки с едой, однако нет шезлонгов и зонтиков (есть только для инвалидов и пенсионеров), народу немного, море чистое. Персонал отеля отзывчивый, попросили 2е одеяло и утюг  сразу принесли. Уборка каждый день, замена полотенец,чай, кофе, вода. В номере есть сейф. Один раз ушли на завтрак не убрав деньги и вещи, оставив сейф открытым, пришли, а у нас уже убрались, при этом ничего не пропало, все проверили и пересчитали.
Отель хоть и 3 звезды тянет скорее на 2+. Номера старые,  поношенные, ковролин с пятнами. Нам не повезло и буквально заселили в номер со старым советскими шкафами (коричневая фанера). В ванной дверца душевой плохо закрывалась и на пол попадала вода. Полотенца хоть и меняли каждый день, были серые. У нас был номер стандарт с 2я отдельными кроватями, но с пометкой, что тип кроватей может измениться, что и произошло, дали номер с одной двуспальной. Если ищете отель для отдыха, то не советую, подойдет для переконтаваться на 1-2 ночи. Мы ездили на экскурсии и почти не бывали в номере, но ощущение осталось совсем не от отеля трешки, несмотря на то, что он вроде как сетевой.

Выбирали городской отель, около метро, чтобы можно было ездить на экскурсии, прогуляться по городу и по желанию сходить на пляж. Метро было в 800м  пешком минут 10, но станция в час пик очень загруженная т.к. рядом находятся несколько бизнес центров, приходилось буквально выпихиваться из вагона. Если попадать не в час пик, то в целом нормально. Около метро есть продуктовый. Из отеля ходит трансфер на пляж Ля Мэр с пн по сб. Мы ходили пешком до общественного пляжа Джумейра 2, около 35 - 40 минут, дорога нормальная, через частный сектор. Пляж цивильный, душевые и туалеты, магазинчики и ларечки с едой, однако нет шезлонгов и зонтиков (есть только для инвалидов и пенсионеров), народу немного, море чистое. Персонал отеля отзывчивый, попросили 2е одеяло и утюг  сразу принесли. Уборка каждый день, замена полотенец,чай, кофе, вода. В номере есть сейф. Один раз ушли на завтрак не убрав деньги и вещи, оставив сейф открытым, пришли, а у нас уже убрались, при этом ничего не пропало, все проверили и пересчитали.
Отель хоть и 3 звезды тянет скорее на 2+. Номера старые,  поношенные, ковролин с пятнами. Нам не повезло и буквально заселили в номер со старым советскими шкафами (коричневая фанера). В ванной дверца душевой плохо закрывалась и на пол попадала вода. Полотенца хоть и меняли каждый день, были серые. У нас был номер стандарт с 2я отдельными кроватями, но с пометкой, что тип кроватей может измениться, что и произошло, дали номер с одной двуспальной. Если ищете отель для отдыха, то не советую, подойдет для переконтаваться на 1-2 ночи. Мы ездили на экскурсии и почти не бывали в номере, но ощущение осталось совсем не от отеля трешки, несмотря на то, что он вроде как сетевой.

Additionally, the front desk person was not very familiar with the amenities diamond members are offered, told me I had to pay for breakfast and didn’t ask if I wanted points or item from the convenience store. All that stuff I can live with because turnover can be a challenge at hotels. What I can’t put up with is dirty rooms, old beds, mold, and peeling paint off the bath tubs. Let’s get into specifics (pictures provided) our room was disgusting the carpets had numerous stains and if you walked bare foot you could feel the grime build up on your feet. The balcony is atrocious, plaster falling off, mold building up. If it wasn’t for driving 8hrs and booking with points I would have checked out immediately and demanded for my money back. Ooo and the best part, their outdoor pool that is open year round and heated is broken and nothing is posted about this on the IHG app or the hotels website. Did I forget to mention I specifically book this hotel for our family of 4 because of the pool. The front desk person did not know  it was down until I asked him about it and he had to ask around to confirm what I said. I stayed at the Holiday Inn Denver East for two nights while in town for a concert, and overall, it was a solid stay. The Uber ride from the airport took about 40 minutes with traffic. Upon arrival, the front desk staff member who checked me in was very friendly and accommodating. I appreciated being able to check in early (for a fee), which was totally worth it.

The room itself was decent — the bed was extremely comfortable, and I liked having a balcony. However, there wasn’t any patio furniture, which would have made it much more enjoyable. The water pressure in the bathroom could have been stronger, but the towels were great quality. Temperature in the room stayed comfortable the whole time, which made it easy to relax.

One of the best features is their complimentary shuttle, which takes you within a 2-mile radius of the hotel. The first driver we had was excellent — very kind and accommodating. Unfortunately, the second driver we encountered during most of our stay was noticeably rude and standoffish, which put a damper on the otherwise helpful service.

I also visited the bar during my stay. The drinks were really good, but the bar staff came across as a bit rude, which was disappointing compared to the quality of the cocktails.

On the positive side, I was granted a late check-out on my last day, which was a huge bonus since I had a later flight. That extra bit of flexibility really stood out.

Would I recommend? Yes — for a quick trip, this hotel is comfortable, well-priced, and has nice amenities. Just know that service experiences may vary.

This resort has potential to be great. The location and proximity to airport transportation is a plus. The views from western facing rooms are spectacular. However, the way this resort is managed and staffed ruined my experience. I have been an IHG platinum elite member for years. I have stayed at Holiday Inns all around the world. I was disappointed by my stay in Denver for a number of reasons. 

Upon arriving, I was not welcomed or greeted as an IHG platinum elite member. I would not have gotten my welcome drink or amenity points had I not asked for them. No attempt to upgrade my stay was made upon arrival.

We finish checking in, and head to our room. We have spent several hours traveling at this point. We are tired and in need of a shower. The shower head was not working properly and there was no hot water. Needless to say, it was unpleasant. We let the front desk know and dealt with it because it was rather late and we were in need of rest. 

Upon waking up in the morning, we realized that the ‘mountain view’ we booked was not given to us. We were placed on the second floor and could not see beyond the trees. 

We were told to call around 11AM the following day to see what alternate rooms were available. We called and were promised a call back (which never happened) Only after calling a second time were we contacted again. Despite chatting with three different staff members across various points of the evening/ morning / afternoon it was not clear if we would be moved to another room. 

We were moved to another room. This one was at least on a higher floor so we could enjoy the view we paid for. However, it reeked of cigarette smoke and the floor was dirty. The fridge was also unexpectedly off, and so our food went bad during our stay. At least the shower was warm so we let those inconveniences slide. 

Lastly, the hotel shuttle system is run inefficiently. The drivers are kind and safe, however they were not immediately outside or near the lobby to transport guests and could only be called by the front desk staff . This increased the wait time and making it difficult to arrive it to transit stops in a timely manner. Despite being in the lobby 10 minutes before our intended departure, we were stuck waiting in the front desk line to notify staff about our shuttle need. The front desk was not adequately staffed, with only one person manning it during morning and check out hours (when it is most active in our opinion) She was trying her best but she needed another person to help. Please staff the desk better during peak hours. The delays caused us to missed the train and had to wait for an extra 20 minutes in the summer heat - delaying our travel activities. 

Despite all of the above, there were some benefits to staying here. The beds were comfortable and the location near the highway made it a great launch point to the rest of the City and area. With some intentional changes, this resort can offer a much better experience to its guests (especially IHG one rewards members who expect better from this chain)

My stay at this hotel was absolutely outstanding, and it truly deserves recognition. From the moment I arrived, I was greeted warmly by the front desk staff, who were professional, attentive, and eager to make me feel welcome. The check-in process was quick and efficient, which was greatly appreciated after a long trip.

The room was spotless, spacious, and extremely comfortable. The bed was exceptionally cozy, with soft sheets and high-quality pillows that guaranteed a restful night’s sleep. The modern yet inviting décor, combined with a peaceful and quiet atmosphere, made my stay even more enjoyable.

The hotel’s location is another major advantage. Conveniently situated in Denver, it provides easy access to local restaurants, shops, and popular attractions. The secure and accessible parking was also a big plus, especially for those traveling by car.

Breakfast exceeded my expectations with a wide variety of fresh, well-prepared options. The restaurant staff were attentive and friendly, ensuring everything was perfect.

Finally, I was impressed by the hotel’s cleanliness and commitment to guest safety and comfort, which gave me a real sense of peace of mind. I highly recommend this hotel to anyone visiting Denver, whether for business or leisure. My experience here was memorable, and I will certainly return on future trips.

However, there were some notable drawback. The towels in the bathroom were rough and felt low-quality, which made them less comfortable to use. Replacing them with softer, higher-quality towels would greatly improve the guest experience. Additionally, the ceiling in my room was peeling, which was visually unappealing and gave the impression of neglected maintenance. 
The hotel lobby was uncomfortably hot during my stay due to a malfunctioning air conditioner, which made spending time in the common areas less pleasant. while not a major inconvenience, the laundry room was also out of service as they were replacing the washer and dryer, so guests needing laundry facilities were out of luck. 
Overall, while the room was sufficient for the price, the rough towels, peeling ceiling, hot lobby, and unavailable laundry room detracted from the experience. With some improvements, this Holiday Inn could offer a more comfortable and enjoyable stay.

This dilapidated ode to airport hotels of a century past lacks A/C in the atrium and restaurant area, lacks functional A/C in the rooms, has poor water pressure, no functional ice machines, and offers few of the amenities IHG members expect.

Want rewards points for paying for parking? Nope. It’s a third party. Want to charge a canned coffee to your room? Nope, it’s a third party.  Ice is a luxury afforded only when the restaurant on site is willing and able.

On the plus side we did not observe the infamous sewage smell reported by others, the beds were comfortable, and the 11th floor views were pretty great.

Just the same, this weathered property and defeated staff should be an avoided by all but the most desperate travelers.  You can’t expect perfection at value pricing, but you should at least have the bare essentials that this Holiday Inn simply cannot provide.

The design inspires vertigo and is super unsafe for little kids. I asked to be moved to the ground floor, was told there are no rooms on it. They put me on the second, and as I walked into the room I was immediately hit by a musty smell- it smelled like someone had been smoking in it. I asked to be switched again, same smell in the next room, and the next. I eventually accepted that the whole hotel just smells dank, as noted in many reviews I subsequently read. The room felt very dirty and dusty. It wasn’t until after my kids were in the bathtub that I noticed a number of hairs of a different color and texture than ours in the water, felt the slimy unclean feeling of the tub, and noticed some blood (!!!) on the side. At breakfast I was chatting with some other guests who all noted similar deficiencies. I was sitting at the restaurant table and glanced behind the TV hanging over my head and found the back absolutely LOADED with what must have been YEARS of dust. The whole place feels like the cleaning crew does the most cursory of jobs hitting some surfaces but not others and there is no time spent cleaning harder to reach places. My kids had been excited about the supposedly heated pool and hot tub and found both freezing. The front desk attendant could not even tell me when asked whether the pool was open “I’m not sure, im new, I can tell you in an hour when my manager gets in.” I stay once a year at ihg hotels because of the free night with the credit card and always find that the quality of the hotels that actually accept the credit is not great, but this one was abysmal and I’ve decided the value of the free night is not worth the hassle. I would literally pay money NOT to have to stay here again.  Also note we adjusted the temperature slightly in the room and the whole thing turned offf, not to come back on. Did not have heat all night.

My stay at the Holiday Inn Denver East from December 30, 2024, to January 2, 2025, was a mix of highs and lows. My wife and I got married at the Denver Courthouse on New Year’s Eve, making this stay a significant part of our special weekend. Unfortunately, the service didn’t quite live up to the occasion.

Walking into the hotel was delightful—the setup is unique, and the atmosphere felt promising. However, the issues began at check-in. Despite requesting a mountain-view room, we were initially assigned a room on the east side of the hotel. After some effort, we were moved from the 6th floor to the 11th floor to room 1100.

Sadly, the room wasn’t problem-free. That evening, we discovered the water pressure and temperature were virtually non-existent, making it impossible to take a proper shower. To add to the frustration, the room phone didn’t work, forcing me to go downstairs to report the issue. The front desk staff logged the problem and assured us it would be addressed. However, despite being gone all day for our wedding on New Year’s Eve, the issue remained unresolved when we returned.

After another report on New Year’s Day, we finally requested a room change. Throughout our stay, no manager reached out to apologize or offer any resolution, which was disappointing. As an IHG member, I usually expect a warm welcome and even small gestures like complimentary snacks, but this experience fell far short of expectations.

On a positive note, we appreciated the hotel’s aesthetics and overall vibe. Despite the poor service during this stay, I would consider returning, but only if there are assurances that similar issues won’t occur. Hopefully, the management can improve its communication and responsiveness to ensure a better experience for future guests.

Negatives:

- The water for the sink would only run warm water. Didn't matter if you let it run for 5 minutes, both the cold and hot handles produced warm water.
- Speaking of warm water, the shower only gave me hot water one time. Every other time it produced only a slightly warmer temperature than the sink tap. Not fun to shower in after a long day of walking. Not unbearable but for sure unacceptable.
- The bar has the strangest hours. I was able to get a drink at 10:30am (it was a long drive) but then it closed at 11am before lunch time. Reopened at 4:30 only to close at 9:30, which is when I returned from the festival and would have liked to get a nightcap. After 10? Sure. But 9:30? In Denver? Kitchen/bar staff shortage would be my guess.
- Now onto the kitchen: My friend decided to get a burger on our last night and told me it was quite possibly the worst burger he's ever had. Bun was too small, meat seemed boiled rather than grilled, everything was super dry. He said it was overall an extremely unpleasant experience. Again, it seemed like a staff shortage may have had to do with this, with inexperienced "kitchen staff" preparing food, which is, again, unacceptable.

Overall, we were more grateful to just lay down in a bed after a long drive than we were grieved by the numerous issues. I hope this wasn't a normal experience and they resolve these issues soon; typically I have had very pleasant experiences with Holiday Inns, but we absolutely did not this last week.

We stayed at this hotel this past weekend with my husband’s family. To say it was atrocious would be a gross understatement. We are IHG loyalists, to the point that my husband is a Diamond member, and I have never been so embarrassed by an IHG property.

First of all, they were supposedly “paving” the parking lot, so they required all guests to park in a garage that was difficult to access and a good 1/4 mile walk from the lobby. This would have been more understandable if: 1. They were ACTUALLY paving the parking lot, and 2. They had given clear instructions on how to access the garage. We asked them upon check-in how to access it, and they just pointed to a QR code on the desk and gave no further information. We figured maybe we could just pay for the parking using that code, and it would give us another code to scan to access the garage. Nope. We had to go back in and ask, and they told us (rather rudely, I might add) that we should have noticed the code written in the envelope with our room keys.

On top of this already-frustrating first impression, there was no air conditioning in the lobby. They had a couple of “coolers” that they tried to pass off as acceptable alternatives. It was upwards of 85 degrees outside, so naturally the lobby became quite warm by the afternoon each day. This, along with a consistent sewage smell, made any time spent in the lobby or bar/restaurant extremely unpleasant.

The ice machines did not work on the majority of floors (we had several rooms across the whole family, and only one party had access to ice on their floor). The front desk did not care, and simply said “sorry,” without offering any solution. Additionally, the vending machines were all sold out the entire stay.

The water in the showers was lukewarm at BEST. The cold water in the faucets did not work at all. This was the case in all of our rooms.

To top it off, we all went to the bar for a nightcap on our final night. I have never experienced such poor service from a bartender in my life. She was inattentive, rude, and had no idea how to make basic cocktails. Someone ordered a lemon drop martini, and they received what appeared to be a vodka soda (this is after she had to google the recipe). It took over 25 minutes to get her attention for a simple glass of wine.

Last but not least, there were multiple foundation cracks in the ceilings and walls, noticeable water damage, disgusting carpets, and the key card readers in the elevators and exterior doors only worked when they felt like it.

Needless to say, we will never return, and I will CERTAINLY not be booking any of my travel clients at this property. What an absolute disgrace to the IHG name.

I applied heat to the area, which revealed two black bugs. I called the front desk and explained the situation, and the staff offered to move me to yet another room. He also referenced a black light, which I assume is used to detect pests, but this further confused me since he had stated the room was thoroughly checked.

Our stay at this hotel in Dubai was much more than a simple visit: it was an experience defined by kindness, attention, and genuine hospitality.

From the very first moment we felt that there was something special here. It is not only about comfort or services—although those are excellent—but about people. Every member of the hotel staff showed extraordinary care, professionalism, and attentiveness throughout our stay. I wish I could remember everyone’s name, because each person, with their smile and willingness to help, contributed to making this experience so enjoyable. Among the faces we will carry with us, I would especially like to thank Marie Anne, who welcomed us every morning at breakfast with sincere kindness and a radiant smile. It was she who enthusiastically introduced me to karak, the wonderfully aromatic spiced tea so beloved in the Emirates—such a small gesture, yet it became a truly special memory of our trip. A heartfelt thank you also to Jasmine at the restaurant, always attentive and gracious; to Kaung, always helpful and considerate; and to Laxman, who took great care in maintaining the floors and ensuring our room was always perfectly clean.

Our room was spacious, bright, and very comfortable, with a completely renovated and modern bathroom. Everything was spotless and thoughtfully designed for the comfort of guests. The Wi-Fi also worked perfectly, which is always greatly appreciated.

We had chosen the half-board option with breakfast and dinner, and it proved to be an excellent choice. Breakfast offered a perfect balance between sweet and savory options every morning, while dinner was always a pleasant discovery with a variety of dishes that changed each evening. A special mention must go to the desserts: every night they were simply delicious and the perfect way to end the day.

The hotel also offers a very convenient underground parking area, particularly useful for those traveling by car. We also appreciated the thoughtful service at the reception desk, where guests can receive complimentary towels if they wish to go to the beach—small details that reveal a genuine care for guests.

Our stay unexpectedly became longer due to flight cancellations related to the Middle East crisis. In what could have been a difficult situation, the hotel staff showed remarkable understanding and generosity: they allowed us to keep the same room and also helped us by reducing the cost of the additional nights. This gesture of kindness meant a great deal to us.

The hotel is also located in a very convenient area and offers a pleasant spa as well as a welcoming bar, perfect for relaxing after a day exploring the city.

There are hotels that simply provide good service.
And then there are places that truly make you feel welcome.

This hotel belongs to the second category.

We will not only remember the comfortable room, the delicious food, or the excellent services. Above all, we will remember the smiles, the kindness, and the feeling of having been genuinely cared for.

And that is what transforms a simple stay… into a memory that stays with you.

When we return to Dubai, we will not look for another hotel.
We will come back here.

Читая положительные отзывы про данный отель, ожидал какого-то подвоха, что не может быть все так хорошо и недорого. Но все-таки у меня остались только положительные впечатления. Так и не понял, почему у данного отеля 2*, хотя, по моему мнению, он ничем не отличается от 3-4*х отелей. Правда, на вывеске на входе нарисовано 3 звездочки.
Заселение произошло моментально, никто не дожидается 14:00. Пару минут и нам вручили ключи и показали в сторону лифта. Никто не провожал, но мы сами во всем разобрались. Русскоговорящего персонала в отеле нет, выручал только гугл-переводчик. Вай-фай бесплатный и ловит везде.
Номер стандартный просторный. В номере двуспальная кровать, диван, длинный стол, стул, ТВ, часы с радио и блютуз, сейф, холодильник, чайник, чашки. Пополнение чайного набора,  уборка номера (если нужно), смена белья и полотенец ежедневно. Сантехника рабочая, но как обычно пишут – «уставшая», были некоторые недостатки, но по просьбе быстро прибегали, и в силу своих возможностей, устраняли. Балконов нет, окна не открываются, кондер настроен на +23 и t не меняется. Еще не хватало небольшого столика у дивана для приема пищи.
Питание без изысков, скромное, но всегда всего хватает. У нас был только завтрак. В ресторане утром были овощи, сыры, кисломолочка, яйца, омлет, сосиски, что-то еще бобовое или картофельное, выпечка, чай, кофемашина, тостер и т.д. Из фруктов яблоки, бананы, апельсины, арбуз, грейпфрут.
Ближайший пляж Ля-Мер находится примерно в 2,2км, мы ходили пешком, занимало примерно 20-30 мин. Вся прилегающая инфраструктура закрыта на реконструкцию (мы так решили). Есть только душ, переодевалки, туалет. Ну само собой песок и море, плюс пальмы и старые лодки на берегу. Вода в море не очень понравилась, как будто затхлая, т.к. залив, нет открытого простора на море. Можно ездить на автобусе на другие пляжи, где веселее, шумнее и красивее.
Поблизости много разных супер- и гипермаркетов, различных ресторанов и кафе на любой вкус и кошелек.
Кстати, хочу отдельно выразить благодарность администрации отеля и туроператору Fun&Sun.  Дело в том, что мы оказались в Дубае во время конфликта с Ираном, и в период обстрела ОАЭ на несколько дней было закрыто небо для полетов, не летали самолеты. И нам на это время без проблем и бесплатно продлили проживание еще на 2 ночи.

I have been staying at Holiday Inn Express properties for years—mainly in Canada and occasionally in Europe. As a frequent business traveler, even though I’m a Hilton Honors member, I’ve always preferred IHG, especially Holiday Inn Express, because the experience has consistently been excellent. Unfortunately, my recent stay in the UAE has been the complete opposite. This has been, by far, the worst Holiday Inn Express experience I’ve ever had.
This trip was meant to be a vacation, and the hotel genuinely affected our enjoyment. As an IHG member, I feel responsible to share honest feedback so that one poor location does not damage the reputation of a brand I’ve trusted for years.
The issues began immediately. The bathroom was untidy, and the shower enclosure was poorly sealed, causing water to flood the floor every time we used it. Despite having a large number of staff, they were difficult to reach and extremely slow to respond. Our TV remote was missing, and when I called reception, no one answered. I eventually went downstairs and found two staff members free, yet no one had responded to my call.
Housekeeping answered the first time I called, but when I tried again just ten minutes later, they stopped responding entirely. I even approached a staff member in the lobby who promised to come to my room within five minutes, but he never showed up.
Another serious concern was safety. When I tried to open the curtains for sunlight, the entire curtain rail fell and hit my son on the head. The crockery provided was also chipped, which is unacceptable and unsafe.
On the first day, our room telephone wasn’t working. I informed reception, and they assured me someone would come to fix it, but no one ever did. We eventually had to fix the wire ourselves. The only positive aspect was that they honored a late checkout, which is standard policy.
I am sharing this feedback not out of bad intention, but out of genuine concern and hope that the hotel improves. IHG has always been my preferred brand, and I want to ensure that one poorly managed property does not reflect negatively on the entire chain.

Our stay was unfortunately disappointing due to several serious maintenance and cleanliness issues across three rooms.

The 1st room
The sliding glass door of the shower was extremely stiff and difficult to move. One side nearly came off and almost fell on our friend, fortunately, she managed to hold it in time. The bathroom mirror had noticeable writing/scratch marks that became clearly visible when steam formed during hot showers. This was quite scary to experience while showering, and it was obvious that the mirror had not been properly cleaned from previous guests.

When we reported this issue at the front desk, the male staff member who received our complaint responded with a very unprofessional, insincere attitude and tone. Although our friend was later moved to another room, the new room also had a broken shower head holder. By then, she was already too exhausted to request another room change.

The 2nd room
The lighting in our room was very dim, as if the bulbs were about to fail, making the room uncomfortably dark. The pillows were placed upside down (clearly visible from the printed text). The sliding bathroom door was also damaged and barely functional, almost falling off the track.

The hair dryer only worked for about 5 minutes before completely shutting down on its own every time. Additionally, throughout our entire stay, both morning and evening, only extremely hot water was available, cold and warm water did not work at all, even after maintenance staff came to check. The problem always returned afterward.

The 3rd room
one of the electrical outlets was pulled out of the wall and clearly unsafe to use.

Overall
The hallway carpet had a very unpleasant odor, and the overall condition of the room felt old, worn out, and poorly maintained.

Positive note
We would like to sincerely compliment the front desk staff who worked the night shift on December 6th. We approached him regarding a late check-out charge detail due to our tour schedule, and he handled our request with kindness, understanding, and professionalism. This was the only truly positive experience during our stay.

Overall, we were extremely disappointed with the room conditions, safety concerns, cleanliness, and maintenance standards. We sincerely hope management takes this feedback seriously for future improvements.

Я был очень приятно удивлен тем, какой прекрасный уровень обслуживания нам здесь предложили. Для «трешки» - это просто супер! Сами номера - чистые, комфортные, есть абсолютно все для того, чтобы нормально пожить. Отдельная благодарность - персоналу! Они всегда готовы помочь, создают теплую и дружелюбную атмосферу. А еще, огромный плюс – нас заселили раньше без всяких проблем! Это реально спасло. Смело рекомендую этот отель всем, кто ищет отличное соотношение цены и качества. Наш номер на первом этаже оказался на удивление просторным. Кровать удобнейшая, а хрустящее, белоснежное белье сделало отдых по-настоящему комфортным. В номере был полный набор для чая/кофе и, что важно, питьевая вода. Ванная комната поражает своими размерами! Правда, немного расстроило отсутствие стеклянной перегородки в душевой, но это мелочи. Уборка ежедневная, поддерживается идеальная чистота. А звукоизоляция здесь просто превосходная, можно было по-настоящему расслабиться и выспаться. Да, окна не открываются, но это, как я понял, особенность многих отелей в ОАЭ. В целом, впечатление от номера осталось исключительно положительным! Завтраки в отеле нас очень порадовали - они были действительно сытными и разнообразными. Был стандартный набор, как везде (разные каши, яйца в разных видах, сосиски, овощи), но его отлично дополнили местными вкусностями (деликатесами). И что круто - персонал прямо моментально все пополнял, поэтому еда всегда была свежей и в наличии. Я был в восторге от свежих фруктов! Были арбузы, бананы, яблоки, и, конечно, финики. На обед и ужин мы обычно ходили в одно проверенное место - кафе "Волга Стар" на улице 2 Декабря. От отеля идти совсем близко, метров 800, наверное. Меню там просто огромное, можно найти что угодно на любой вкус. Мне особенно запомнились их креветки и рыба, которую подавали прямо на сковороде - это было невероятно вкусно! Порции реально большие, и что самое главное - цены адекватные. Особенно если сравнивать с тем, что берут в кафешках в центре или на Дубай Марина. Рекомендую! Пляж Ля Мер оказался удобно расположенным - мы доходили до него буквально за 20 минут неспешным шагом от отеля. Сам пляж чистенький, ухоженный, с хорошей инфраструктурой. Сразу скажу, если сравнивать с пляжами в Рас-аль-Хайме (там мы были раньше), то они нас впечатлили, пожалуй, чуть больше. Но, если вы ищете место, чтобы нормально отдохнуть, не отъезжая далеко от своего дубайского отеля, Ля Мер - отличный вариант. В эту поездку мы решили сменить обстановку и вместо привычного пляжного отеля выбрали городской в Дубае. И ни капли не пожалели! Мы не просто валялись на пляже, а посетили кучу классных мест: брали обзорку по Дубаю, сходили в Музей Будущего (это просто восторг!) и даже съездили в Абу-Даби. Все эти экскурсии мы брали у ребят из topdubai online. Найти их сайт по названию в интернете не составит труда. За четыре года наших поездок по Эмиратам, могу сказать, что этот отпуск был самым-самым насыщенным. После довольно спокойного отдыха в Шардже и Рас-аль-Хайме, Дубай своей динамикой и разнообразием нас прямо приятно удивил!

Thanks kayan and hesham

I stayed at this hotel for seven days at the beginning of October 2025. The room and staff were all good. BUT: I booked a massage in the spa area. The spa area is presumably a separate company.
