I had a very strange feeling. She told me to turn around. Suddenly, she had my genitals in her hand. She moaned in my ear and tried to sell me sex for 2500 AED. She pressured me to go to the bank and leave my phone as collateral. I was very overwhelmed and refused several times, saying I would think about it so I could get out of the room. She then asked for a 300 AED tip. I gave her the 300 AED to get out quickly. The hotel probably knows nothing about this, as such things are illegal and punishable in Dubai. I didn't inform the hotel because my English isn't very good, and the masseuse would probably have presented it differently. They don't want any trouble with the police in Dubai for a sexual act. I'm not usually picky about hotels, but this place had a ton of issues.

First, you can't control the temperature in your room. When we arrived, the AC was blasting cold air 24/7 even though it was snowing outside. To change the temperature, we had to go down to talk to the front desk (because the phone in our room was broken) and ask them to send the handy man in, who came by a couple hours later. He made some adjustments and then the unit was blasting hot air 24/7 instead. We had to go to the desk again, who promised the maintenance guy would come back in 10 minutes, but over an hour later he finally made another adjustment, and it was only semi-comfortable. Why not just let us control the thermostat in the rooms? This is a solved problem that even the crappiest hotels get right.

Second, they let the free breakfast run out of items well before closing time. I went down 30 minutes before end of breakfast on a Saturday and they were out of pancakes, cinnamon rolls, and apple juice. Also they have only two slow coffee machines for a giant hotel, and there's always a long line to use them. Just have pre-made coffee like everyone else, it's not like the machines will even make a latte.

Third, the toilet wouldn't flush. They have a handle that can be pushed in any direction, but none of the directions seemed to work. Turns out you have to push the handle straight into the wall with a lot of force. If you're going to have a weird flushing handle, why not at least have a sign explaining how it works?

Fourth, when we arrived a little after midnight, we had to wait 20 minutes for them to be able to make a room key because their system was rebooting.

Fifth, the only parking is $50 valet parking ... for a Holiday Inn. Thankfully if you get the spot hero app you can find drastically cheaper parking nearby, but you shouldn't have to.

This was an extremely frustrating stay for me. I stayed at the Holiday Inn in Denver for 5 nights and every single night I had multiple power outages. The power was out when I first arrived at the room and it took over an hour for it to be fixed. Even on days when we got back to the room late, the power went out multiple times and we were sitting in the dark for 30+ minutes at a time. Billy at the front desk was kind enough to offer compensation for the issues during our stay, and told us to have the third party booking service reach out to him. The third party has tried reaching out to the hotel multiple times throughout the last week with absolutely no response from the hotel. The power outage issues alone were extremely frustrating, but dodging phone calls and emails (sent directly to the manager) to avoid taking responsibility is even more so unacceptable.

Stayed at Holiday Inn Downtown Denver for a 3-day business trip, and the location was extremely convenient for getting around the city.

Valet parking is available, but at $51 per night, it felt a bit steep. We opted for a nearby public parking garage just up the street, which was still paid parking—but significantly more affordable and an easy alternative.

Breakfast is simple and self-serve. Nothing standout, but it gets the job done—perfect for grabbing a quick coffee and bagel before heading out for the day.

Overall, this is a solid, practical choice for a business stay. Clean, convenient, and easy to navigate. I’d definitely stay here again when traveling to Denver for work.

My room was the opposite...I froze..literally slept under the blankets with my jacket on cold. The building thermostat is centrally controlled (really, in 2026). The room wasn't bad. A few cosmetic issues. But when I woke up, reality hit. Not only was the room freezing ,but no hot water in the shower, toilet barely flushed, tub clogged. I just washed off over the sink, grabbed breakfast, and had to leave. I informed the desk clerk but what will happen, I don't know. The walls are super thin. I heard my neighbors (both sides) complaining that they didn't have hot water. So maybe it's the entire 9th floor. Other customers were complaining about the sketchy elevator. Sometimes it won't let you pick a floor. All in all, I say; stay at your own risk...the reviews are real...but you cant beat the location to 16th street and Union Station.

Overall, not a satisfying stay.

First I'll say the staff was/is excellent, 5 stars, particulary Gianni (? may be misspelling his name), who was responsive & understanding at the front desk. I stay at HI Express for business travel a lot because it's a solid, reliable hotel room with little variation, so you always know what you're getting. But this hotel is nothing like that. (A) The major problem that caused me to leave one night early is NO CONTROL of in-room temperature/air. Apparently the hotel is old and all STEAM, so during a warm February spell, the rooms bake. I didn't get the room lower than 73 degrees my entire stay even with maintenance fiddling with the unit. (B) The rooms are oddly laid out. It was a nice room, but frustrating for 100 reasons. No outlets handy for charging. Irregular shapes. A toilet bowl set diagonally in the bathroom. Nowhere to hang anything except the closet, no hooks. It didn't feel like a HI Express Room. The Good:
Excellent location in downtown Denver — just a 10-minute drive to Union Station and surrounded by plenty of restaurants. The area feels safe and well-lit even at night. The hotel is clean, tidy, and modern, with a professional vibe. They have a 24-hour kitchen stocked with coffee and drinking water, and most rooms come with a microwave, which is convenient. The staff is friendly and helpful.

The Not-So-Good:
The reception never picks up the phone, which can be frustrating. There’s no airport shuttle service, and no complimentary water bottles in the room — though that’s understandable for a value hotel. Also, there aren’t many convenience stores nearby.

Incident:
Unfortunately, the metal frame arm under the bed had come loose and scratched my leg, even tearing my pants due to its sharp edge. The staff assured me they would compensate for the damage. I’ll update this review once that is resolved.

I had to put it on the floor if I wanted to use it.  Turned out it didn't work anyway.  Others have complained about the thermostat, but I didn't have a problem. I’d like to take a moment to appreciate the wonderful front desk team for making my stay so special. The professionalism and warmth truly stood out. A special mention to Leo, who recognized my membership right away and made the check-in process incredibly fast and smooth.
Kevin from in-room dining was equally impressive—his recommendations were spot on! We tried the traditional Indian curry, and the authentic flavors instantly reminded me of Mumbai. Absolutely delicious! 🍛
The breakfast spread was fantastic, offering a wide variety of options to suit every taste. I’d also like to thank Sumana for her excellent service over the phone and for arranging a room with a beautiful view. The fruit platter was a lovely touch that made the stay even more memorable. 🍓
The hotel is definitely a great choice for both short and long stays—budget-friendly, conveniently located just a 5–7 minute walk from the metro, and featuring in-room dining, a restaurant, Muchachas outlet, and a rooftop bar. Complimentary breakfast was truly the cherry on top!

As an IHG Gold member since 2001, I’ve stayed at IHG properties for over 25 years. Unfortunately, my recent stay at Holiday Inn Express DIC was the worst hotel experience I’ve had during that time.

The room cleanliness was unacceptable, with visible stains in several areas. This immediately lowered my confidence in the property’s standards.

The breakfast experience was even more disappointing. Although breakfast officially runs until 10:30 a.m., staff begin clearing and shutting down the area earlier. By 10:30 sharp, guests are prevented from even using the coffee machines. I was stopped from getting a coffee exactly at 10:30 a.m., which felt unnecessary and unwelcoming.

I raised the issue with both the Restaurant Manager and the General Manager, but their response lacked accountability and guest focus, which was surprising and disappointing.

After decades of loyalty to IHG, I expected much better. This property does not reflect the service standards I associate with the brand.

Had a great stay. The experience was very good. Special thanks to Leo for upgrading my room from the first floor to the third floor, with an amazing view of Atlantis, Burj Al Arab, and Burj Khalifa. Breakfast was also superb. Overall, a comfortable and pleasant stay.

I stayed at the Holiday Inn Express in Dubai Internet City for 12 nights in January 2026 and had a pleasant experience overall. The room was comfortable, well-maintained, and had all the essential amenities needed for a long stay. The bed was cozy, the air‑conditioning worked perfectly, and the room layout was practical for both work and relaxation.

The breakfast buffet was one of the highlights of my stay. There were more than 15 items to choose from every morning, including hot dishes, pastries, fresh fruits, cereals, and beverages. The food was consistently tasty and filling, and the staff managing the breakfast area were always friendly and attentive.

I also used the mini gym once during my stay. It’s not very large, but it’s clean and has enough equipment for a quick workout. For a budget-friendly hotel, it does the job well.

The location is another big plus. The hotel is just a short walk from both the Dubai Internet City metro station and the tram station, making it very convenient to get around the city. The surrounding neighborhood is peaceful, less crowded than other parts of Dubai, and has a nice, clean atmosphere with cafes and stores nearby.

Housekeeping deserves special appreciation. My room was cleaned daily by Maulana, who did an excellent job throughout my stay. The room was always spotless, towels replaced, and supplies replenished without fail.

I did experience one issue during my stay — loud music from the open bar on one of the nights, which made it difficult to sleep. However, the staff handled it exceptionally well. Leo from the front desk immediately changed my room to a quieter area as soon as I raised the concern. Thanks to his quick support, the rest of my nights were peaceful.

Overall, this hotel offers great value for money, a comfortable stay, good food, and a very helpful staff. I would definitely consider staying here again on my next visit to Dubai.

На самом деле отель превзошел ожидания. Хороший сервис. Питание достаточно разнообразное и в целом вкусное, особенно ужины. Отзывчивый персонал, помогали решать все вопросы. Достаточно чисто.
Локация в целом хорошая, но до пляжа и прогулочных мест нужно ехать на такси или метро. Марина 10-15 минут на такси. Метро минут 7 ходьбы. Пляж пешком далеко, либо Jumeira/Kite, либо JBR. Ехать на такси. Есть бесплатный трансфер от отеля на Jumeira. Если будете просить номер повыше, уточните курящий этаж или нет. Мы жили на 4-ом, этаж оказался курящий. По началу был слегка неприятный запах, потом привыкли. На 5 этаже находится какой-то банкетный зал или рестик, там по выходным вечером и ночью шумно. А вид прикольный. Номера в целом норм, но уже чувствуется что давно не обновляли, есть пятна и мелкие недочеты. Но, за эту цену, все круто. Постель классная, как в дорогих сетевых отелях. Спасибо за гостеприимство!

We stayed at Holiday Inn Dubai Internet City, and overall our experience was very positive.

The breakfasts are filling and offer a decent variety - you definitely won’t stay hungry, although personally we missed having a bit more meat options.

The service is excellent: the room is cleaned every day, everything is kept very tidy, and towels are changed regularly.

We would especially like to mention Rachel at the reception. She was a pleasure to interact with, helped us with all our questions despite the language barrier, and made our stay very comfortable.

The location is very convenient: you can easily reach key points of the city by any type of public transport, and two metro lines are within a 10-minute walk from the hotel.

The only downside for us was that the windows in our room did not open, so sometimes there was a stale smell. However, it would usually disappear by the next day.

Overall, for its price category, this hotel is an excellent choice and can definitely be recommended.

Rachel, отличный человек, пошла нам на встречу, разрешила остаться до 15.00 бесплатно, нам с мужем очень понравилось расположение отеля, шведский стол хороший, наедались, всё вкусно, постель меняли постоянно, рядом рестораны, автобус 84 и трамвай до JBR пляж, рекомендую, спасибо всем в отеле за тёплый приём.
